rationale: The citing paper uses Kivetz and Simonson (2002) as theory support for the claim that utilitarian concerns dominate until consumers feel justified to indulge. That idea is integrated into the paper's conceptual setup for why form incongruity and positioning should shift hedonic versus utilitarian benefit perceptions and evaluations. The paper also revisits this citation in the discussion to position its contribution as adding a new condition for functional-versus-hedonic preference reversals, so the reference is more than a passing background mention.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on the 'earning the right to indulge' logic from Kivetz and Simonson (2002) as a conceptual basis for its theorizing about utilitarian-first processing and later hedonic evaluation. It then positions its own findings on product form and positioning as adding to that broader hedonic-versus-utilitarian preference reversal literature.
